title: erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. neomexicanus
intro: Erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. neomexicanus, also known as New Mexican fleabane, is a perennial flowering plant native to New Mexico in the United States. It is a member of the daisy family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Herbal preparations of Erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. neomexicanus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
